claude_reasoning.reasoning: COGS is negative (-87.8M) when it should be positive. This appears to be a sign error in extraction - COGS should represent costs, not negative costs.
This is a cascading effect from the negative COGS. If COGS were positive 87.8M, then Revenue (148.5M) - COGS (87.8M) = 60.7M gross profit, which matches the actual value.
claude_reasoning.suggested_fix: Fix COGS sign error, then gross profit calculation will be correct

claude_reasoning.reasoning: Effective tax rate shows 250000.00 (25M) instead of a percentage. This appears to be a units error - likely should be 0.25 (25%).
Same cascading issue as flag 1 - fixing the COGS sign error will resolve this gross profit calculation error.
Net income (12.4M) vs expected (20.6M) suggests tax expense calculation issue. With EBT of 16.5M and tax expense of 4.1M, net income should be 12.4M, so the expected value calculation may be wrong.
claude_reasoning.suggested_fix: Verify tax expense and EBT values - current calculation appears correct

claude_reasoning.reasoning: Gross margin shows 409000.00 (409K) instead of a ratio. This should be gross profit / revenue = 60.7M / 148.5M ≈ 0.409.
Same issue as flag 5 - gross margin is stored as absolute value instead of ratio/percentage.
EBT difference could be due to other non-operating items not captured in the simple EBIT - interest calculation. Warning level is appropriate.
